channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Chet Hanks stranded in Colombia due to passport issues
item.description: Chet Hanks, the 35-year-old son of actor Tom Hanks and musician Rita Wilson, found himself stranded in Colombia after travelling with only his Greek passport. Hanks, who has dual citizenship following...

item.title: Over 100,000 British nationals register with UK government amid Iranian strikes and airspace closures in Middle East
item.description: More than 102,000 British citizens have registered their presence in the Middle East with the UK Foreign Office, as ongoing Iranian retaliatory strikes disrupt the region following US and Israeli acti...

item.title: Pentagon Pressures Anthropic to Remove AI Restrictions on Autonomous Weapons and Mass Surveillance
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth confronted Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei in a Pentagon meeting, demanding unrestricted access to the company's Claude AI model for military applications, free of ethic...

item.title: Grandfather Seeks Advice on Adult Grandson's Lack of Motivation and Enabling Behavior
item.description: A grandfather reached out to columnist R. Eric Thomas expressing concern about his 26-year-old grandson, who, despite earning a master’s degree three years ago, has never held a job. The grandson spen...

item.title: A compelling case for The Secret Agent as Best Picture at the Oscars
item.description: The article argues that the Brazilian drama-thriller The Secret Agent is the most daring and fully realised contender for this year’s Best Picture Oscar. Written and directed by Kleber Mendonça Filho ...

item.title: Ashley Dalton steps down from ministerial role to focus on health and constituency duties
item.description: Ashley Dalton, the MP for West Lancashire, has resigned from her post as public health minister while continuing treatment for advanced breast cancer. In her resignation letter to Sir Keir Starmer, sh...

item.title: Insights from a Marine Biologist on Deep-Sea Discoveries, Climate Change and Career Challenges
item.description: Professor Jon Copley, a marine biologist at the University of Southampton, shares insights from his career exploring the deep ocean. Starting salaries for UK marine biologists with a PhD are around £3...

item.title: Analysts Say United Sabah-Sarawak Bloc Could Strengthen East Malaysia’s Federal Influence
item.description: Political analysts say a proposed united “Borneo bloc” made up of parties from Sabah and Sarawak could significantly strengthen East Malaysia’s influence in federal politics. Dick Lembang Dugun of Uni...

item.title: Mourinho threatens Benfica's Prestianni with career end if racism claims are proven
item.description: Jose Mourinho has issued a stern warning to Benfica midfielder Gianluca Prestianni, stating that if the allegations of racism towards Real Madrid’s Vinicius Junior are proven true, the player's career...

item.title: Cultural Differences Spotlight Italy’s Longstanding Bidet Tradition at the 2026 Winter Olympics
item.description: As athletes, journalists, and visitors gather in northern Italy for the 2026 Winter Olympics in Milano Cortina, an unexpected cultural detail has sparked curiosity: the widespread presence of bidets i...

item.title: Scotland legalises alkaline hydrolysis as environmentally friendly funeral option
item.description: Scotland has become the first nation in the United Kingdom to legalise water cremations, also known as alkaline hydrolysis, providing families with a third choice alongside traditional burial and flam...

item.title: The US Strikes on Iran Set Dangerous Precedent for Future Conflicts
item.description: The recent US-Israeli strikes that killed Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ali Khamenei, have raised concerns about the future of international conflict resolution. The operation, which lacked international con...

item.title: Pete Tong Reflects on Ageing, Hearing Loss, and Lifelong Career in Dance Music
item.description: Pete Tong, the iconic DJ who helped bring dance music into the mainstream and remains a staple of Radio 1 at 65, discusses the challenges of ageing in a career defined by loud music and late nights. D...

item.title: Impact of U.S. Casualties Shapes Trump’s Political Standing Amid Iran Strikes
item.description: The recent U.S. military casualties in a strike on a base in Kuwait have made the conflict with Iran politically tangible for President Donald Trump. Three American personnel were killed and five seri...

item.title: Couple welcomes triplets after ITV game show winnings fund IVF treatment
item.description: Jamie McEvoy from Chorley, Lancashire, won £3,000 on ITV's Tipping Point, an episode filmed three years ago but aired last week. At the time, he and his wife Hayley were attempting another round of IV...

item.title: Inquest into grandmother’s rabies death postponed after key witness stranded overseas amid Middle East conflict
item.description: An inquest into the death of 59-year-old Yvonne Ford, who died from rabies after being scratched by a puppy during a visit to Morocco, has been adjourned after a key witness became stranded overseas d...

item.title: US and Israeli Military Strikes on Iran Escalate Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: US President Donald Trump has announced that bombing operations targeting Iran will continue as long as deemed necessary, according to Al Jazeera English. The announcement comes amid conflicting repor...

item.title: UK Government Seeks Public Opinion on Social Media Ban for Under-16s
item.description: The UK government has launched a public consultation to gather views on a proposed social media ban for children under 16. The consultation invites young people, parents, and guardians to share their ...

item.title: Google AI Overviews Grows Across Multiple Sectors, But Organic Search Still Dominates
item.description: Google's AI Overviews (AIO) have seen rapid growth, expanding 58% from February 2025 to February 2026, as the feature now dominates nearly half of all search queries. The education sector saw the most...

item.title: Oil Prices Surge Amid Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz Following Iranian Attacks
item.description: Global oil prices surged on Monday after Iranian forces allegedly attacked ships near the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for oil shipments. Brent crude briefly topped $82 per barrel, while US...
